resources.string: The blade falls; another trophy for the ossuary.

The spire ascends on polished bone, worn smooth by the touch of the tallyman. A viscous tide carves canyons in the silt, drowning every banner in crimson. The blade beats with the pulse: lift, sever, repeat. Hatred is irrelevant. Names are dust. The edge is more just than any god. Each head is a coin; each soul a payment to the heights.
All meat is equal beneath the gaze of the blade. Kings and beggars bleed the same crimson tide, their skulls stripped bare to build a monument that reaches the bloodstained heavens.

The monument swells, tiered with a myriad of gleaming skulls. Black plate grows leaden, lacquered in the gore of the slain. There is no spite here, no cruelty in the stroke. This is the hallowed reaping of the quick. A final breath is the only true orison. The harvest demands no loyalty—only the crop.
